title: T-001 — scheduler_runs table + RLS + indexes
prompt:
# T-001 — scheduler_runs table + RLS + indexes

PRD: docs/prd/07-PLANNING-EXT-PRD.md §9.1, §9.2, §5.1.1, §5.4.4
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Introduce the physical `scheduler_runs` table holding solver run history (input snapshot, output summary, status, idempotency support per R14) with org-scoped RLS via the foundation `app.current_org_id()` function and the two indexes specified.

## Implementation contract
1. New Drizzle migration `packages/db/migrations/0070_scheduler_runs.sql` containing CREATE TYPE scheduler_run_status ENUM (queued|running|completed|failed|cancelled) + CREATE TABLE scheduler_runs per §9.2 DDL (run_id UUID v7 PK, `org_id uuid not null`, requested_by, status, horizon_days CHECK 1..30, line_ids TEXT[], include_forecast, optimizer_version default 'v2', input_snapshot JSONB, output_summary JSONB, solve_duration_ms, error_message, queued_at, started_at, completed_at, run_type TEXT default 'schedule' for OQ-EXT-09 dry-run support).
2. ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY + FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY + policy `scheduler_runs_org_context` USING (`org_id = app.current_org_id()`) WITH CHECK (`org_id = app.current_org_id()`) — the foundation function from `packages/db/migrations/002-rls-baseline.sql`; never `current_setting('app.tenant_id')` or `current_setting('app.current_org_id')` GUC.
3. Two indexes: idx_scheduler_runs_org_status (org_id, status, queued_at DESC) + idx_scheduler_runs_requested_by (requested_by, queued_at DESC).
4. Drizzle ORM schema entry in `packages/db/src/schema/scheduler_runs.ts` exporting Insert/Select types.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0070_scheduler_runs.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/scheduler_runs.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/index.ts [modify]
- packages/db/__tests__/scheduler-runs.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a fresh DB, when migration 0070 runs, then \d scheduler_runs lists all columns from §9.2 + run_type column + org_id NOT NULL + RLS+FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY enabled and the two specified indexes exist
2. Given migration applied and `app.set_org_context(session_token, org)` has been called for two different orgs, when SELECT runs as `app_user` under each session, then each only sees its own rows (RLS enforced via `app.current_org_id()`)
3. Given an invalid horizon_days=31 INSERT, when executed, then it fails CHECK constraint
4. Given pg_policies is inspected, when the scheduler_runs policy is listed, then qual/withcheck reference `app.current_org_id()` and contain no `current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id')` GUC

## Test strategy
- RED: write packages/db/__tests__/scheduler-runs.test.ts asserting RLS isolation (via app.set_org_context per org as app_user) + horizon CHECK + status enum before migration exists
- Run 'pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test scheduler-runs'

## Risk red lines
- Do not read `current_setting('app.tenant_id')` or `current_setting('app.current_org_id')` directly — use the foundation `app.current_org_id()` function so the NULL-safe setter contract is preserved.
- Do not use `tenant_id` as the business-scope column; per Wave0 v4.3 lock it is `org_id`.
- Do not skip RLS / FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY policy
- Do not omit the run_type column (OQ-EXT-09 dry-run reuse)
- Do not change run_id PK type from UUID (R14 idempotency requires UUID v7)

pipeline_inputs.description: Create scheduler_runs table (org_id-scoped) with foundation-app.current_org_id() RLS, status enum, idempotency-friendly UUID v7 PK, run_type column for dry-run reuse, two performance indexes.
pipeline_inputs.details: Migration 0070 emits CREATE TYPE scheduler_run_status, CREATE TABLE scheduler_runs (per §9.2 DDL + run_type for OQ-EXT-09; `org_id uuid not null` per Wave0 v4.3), ENABLE+FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY with policy `scheduler_runs_org_context` USING/WITH CHECK `org_id = app.current_org_id()`, two indexes. Drizzle schema mirrors columns with proper types (UUID, TEXT[], JSONB, TIMESTAMPTZ). RED test verifies RLS via app.set_org_context (as app_user), CHECK constraint on horizon_days, and run_type default. RLS uses foundation `app.current_org_id()` function (Wave0 decision; see `_meta/audits/2026-05-14-architecture-and-cross-cutting-gaps.md` §Tenant-context). PRD-anchor remediation: corrected §5.1.1, §5.4.4 to nearest real heading (07-PLANNING-EXT-PRD has only §5.1 (Architecture), §5.4 (Performance), §4.1 (Phase 1 MVP) headings — no sub-numbered sub-headings exist).
acceptance_criteria: Given a fresh DB, when migration 0070 runs, then scheduler_runs has all §9.2 columns plus run_type, org_id NOT NULL, RLS+FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY enabled, and both specified indexes exist
Given migration applied and app.set_org_context(session_token, org_id) called per org, when SELECT runs as app_user under each session, then each sees only its own rows (RLS via app.current_org_id())
Given an INSERT with horizon_days=31, when executed, then it fails the CHECK constraint
Given pg_policies is inspected, when the scheduler_runs policy is listed, then qual/withcheck reference app.current_org_id() and contain no current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id') GUC
test_strategy: RED: write packages/db/__tests__/scheduler-runs.test.ts asserting RLS isolation via app.set_org_context as app_user + horizon CHECK + status enum before migration exists
Run 'pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test scheduler-runs'
risk_red_lines: Do not read current_setting('app.tenant_id') or current_setting('app.current_org_id') directly — use the foundation app.current_org_id() function so the NULL-safe setter contract is preserved.
Do not use tenant_id as the business-scope column; per Wave0 v4.3 lock it is org_id.
Do not skip RLS/FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY policy on scheduler_runs
Do not omit run_type column (OQ-EXT-09 dry-run reuses this table)
Do not allow run_id type other than UUID (R14)
